explanations.23: SHA256 hash is a way to create a unique fingerprint of the file content; if the file hasn't changed, the hash stays the same, so we can recognize it in the cache.
explanations.28: On startup means when the backend service first starts up; the backend needs to scan all Markdown files once and send them to Meilisearch so they become searchable.
explanations.53: The `prefers-color-scheme` is a browser setting that tells us whether the user's operating system is set to dark mode or light mode, and we should respect that choice automatically.
explanations.64: A multi-stage build means we use one Docker image to compile/prepare the code, then copy only the final result into a smaller image, so the deployed container isn't bloated with build tools.
explanations.70: The `:ro` at the end means read-only; the backend can read the documentation files but cannot modify or delete them.
